item.title: Dirty Dancing
item.description: When we think about Dirty Dancing, we often picture the bottom image. We forget that the whole reason Baby has to fill in for Penny is because Robby gets Penny pregnant and refuses to take responsibility. We forget that the abortion is so expensive Penny cannot afford it, even from a questionable practitioner, and when Robby refuses to pay for it, Baby has to get the money for her. We forget that Penny nearly dies because abortions are illegal. We forget the horrible conditions she is forced to endure in order to get the abortion and the incredible pain she was in afterwards. We forget her tears. We forget her shame. We forget the judgment and condemnation towards her. We forget Robby’s endorsement for college and smug attitude as his life remained unchanged. While this is a movie, there have been real life Pennys. We must not return to the time where this is the norm. Abortions have never been an issue for the wealthy. Money affords opportunity and secrecy. The United States has the highest maternal mortality rate amongst industrialized countries. Banning abortions is not going to help that number.
item.dc:creator: psuedon

item.title: Remember the Victims.
item.description: My city is grieving. Please remember these brave people, not the monster who caused so much heartbreak. We are a city of love, The City of Good Neighbors.

item.title: Here are 5 more movies that I enjoyed the heck out of and you might too
item.description:
man, this film pulled me right out of my reality and into it's own. The story is Split between the parallel realities of contemporary London and the otherworldly metropolis of Meanwhile City, Ryan phillipe plays a masked vigilante detective... if you like a little bit of mystery and mind bending in your movies this is for you. plus it has Eva green in it, I should have started with that
This is one of the greatest things I have ever watched, hands down. There is little to no dialogue, everything is brought to you with sounds and music and boy is it impactful. It's the story of champion, a boy who dreams of being a cyclist in the tour de france and his grandma. incredible film and an all time favorite of mine.
I have seen this film a dozen times and I love it more every time, one of Sandra bullocks more underrated films I think. she's an adict who gets sent to rehab, there's a delightful character played by Alan Tudyk. it's funny, it's sad and it's generally great.
This film feels like Michael Caine and Robert Duvall gave you a big hug through the screen. I mean, honestly that should be enough for you to go watch it. it's a really interesting film, it follows the story of Walter, a young boy who gets abandoned with his uncles for the summer. there's a story within a story as the uncles begin to tell Walter about their life. I absolutely adore this film, it's got a real feel good vibe to it.
This was one of the first films I saw that left me feeling like I'd been removed from my life, taken on a ride and then plonked back. I was stunned after I watched this in a very delightfully odd surreal way. it's the story of a lifelong rivalry between 2 free divers. its a bit of a cult classic now, very 80's and totally brilliant.
item.dc:creator: rachc50142
